claim: Confirmed: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API supports external research/fetch evidence with proper scope separation, and project facts require project-scoped path evidence.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines the four allowed scope categories: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: Establishes the foundation of the scope separation system

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard-codes requirements: every evidence record must include scope; project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths; external research must be marked scope=external
evidence.why: Direct enforcement of scope declaration and path requirements in agent contracts

evidence.summary: Explicit protocol: when demand asks for external research, use WebFetch/WebSearch/MCP web reader and mark records as scope=external; if no such tool available, put in missing
evidence.why: Defines the exact workflow for external research evidence collection

evidence.summary: Tool-profile-aware instructions: research profile agents get explicit guidance that web/fetch tools are for external evidence and must not be used as project facts
evidence.why: Ensures agents with research tool profiles understand scope boundaries

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function implements comprehensive scope classification: checks explicit scope field, infers from URLs, source types, and path presence
evidence.why: Core runtime logic that separates external from project evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope validates that records explicitly declare scope as one of the four valid categories
evidence.why: Validation gate that blocks evidence without proper scope declaration

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues enforces that existing-project facts must have project-scoped evidence with concrete path locators; blocks project-scoped records without paths or facts with only external/user/unknown evidence
evidence.why: Critical validation preventing external evidence from satisfying project fact requirements

evidence.summary: PRD readiness validation integrates scope checking: calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for all completed evidence results
evidence.why: Ensures scope validation is enforced at the PRD readiness gate

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states: project evidence must have repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence can only be background research and cannot alone prove project facts
evidence.why: Product-level contract that external research cannot substitute for project-scoped evidence

evidence.summary: Dispatch safety model: agents have full tool access but harness boundary audit detects any target-project mutation; only writes to evidence artifact root allowed
evidence.why: Explains how the system allows external research tools while preventing project modifications

risks: If scope validation logic has bugs, external evidence could incorrectly satisfy project fact requirements
Agents might misinterpret tool profile instructions and use external research tools as if they were project evidence

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all 10 explorer evidence points. The implementation correctly separates external research (scope=external) from project facts (scope=project with paths). Agent prompts (evidence-dispatch.ts:316-320), validation functions (router.ts:438-447, 513-530), and doctrine (docs/yolo-demand-doctrine.md:70) all align on the scope separation contract. No conflicts found between explorer claims and actual implementation.
